text:
There once was an old oak tree. This tree had seen many winters and the occasional summer bloom. Its gnarled trunk carried vines and moss, and its ancient branches swayed with the passing breeze.

One day, a little girl with ginger curls and brown eyes discovered the tree. She sat under its broad shade, reading a book. Every so often, she would glance up to watch the leaves dance in the gentle rustle.

She leaned closer to the tree and whispered, "Hello old friend. You're quite fascinating."

The tree hummed softly. "Ah," it responded. "So you've come to talk? I've listened to stories of your children."

The girl nodded, her cheeks flushed with excitement. "Really? You've got grandchildren?"

The tree shook its leaves slightly. "No. But you might learn a thing or two about growth." Its voice was deep and ancient.

"How?" she asked, intrigued.
